source_item.title: Yes Bank Q1 Results: Net profit surges 34% YoY to Rs 1,071 crore; NII advances 18%

slide1_hook.label: Yes Bank Is Back. Numbers Prove It.
slide1_hook.subhead: The bank that nearly died just posted a strong profit.

slide2_backstory.label: THE BACKSTORY
slide2_backstory.headline: From ₹370 Stock to ₹50,000 Withdrawal Cap
slide2_backstory.body: Yes Bank's stock once hit ₹370 in 2018, back when it was India's fastest-growing private bank. Bad loans and fleeing deposits forced the RBI to cap withdrawals at ₹50,000 in 2020, until SBI led a ₹10,000 crore bailout.

slide3_news.label: THE UPDATE
slide3_news.headline: 34% Profit Jump. Ratings Upgraded. Margins Hold.
slide3_news.body: Yes Bank's profit jumped 34% to Rs 1,071 crore this quarter, and net interest income grew 17.5%. Bad loans kept falling, and Moody's, CARE, ICRA, and now S&P all upgraded its ratings.

slide4_why.label: WHY IT MATTERS
slide4_why.headline: This Turnaround Changes What's Possible Next
bullets: Japan's SMBC now owns a 24% stake
Better ratings mean cheaper funding ahead
Proof that a near-collapsed bank can recover

slide5_engage.label: YOUR TAKE
slide5_engage.headline: SBI and other Indian banks bailed out Yes Bank in 2020, and now a Japanese bank is its biggest shareholder. Is that proof India's banking system is maturing, or did the rescue just hand a prize asset to foreign capital?
slide5_engage.cta: Follow for daily Indian business stories explained simply. Drop your take in the comments 👇
